summary: The migration endpoint was identified as allowing unauthenticated access with CSRF protection disabled through code analysis. Docker-based reproduction was not completed due to base image download timeout, but the vulnerability is confirmed by direct inspection of the source code.
expected_security_impact: An unauthenticated POST to /actions/app/migrate should be accepted and processed, potentially enabling maintenance mode, triggering database backups, or running pending migrations.
actual_observed_result: Docker build did not complete within the session time limit due to network-layer image pull timeouts. Code analysis confirms the vulnerability: AppController.php line 66 allows anonymous access to the migrate action, and lines 92-94 disable CSRF validation.
verification_method: The reproduction bundle verifies the vulnerability by: (1) setting up Craft CMS in Docker, (2) sending an unauthenticated POST to /actions/app/migrate, and (3) checking the response for confirmation that the migration controller was reached without authentication.
evidence_produced_by_run: Source code confirms 'migrate' is in $allowAnonymous with ALLOW_ANONYMOUS_LIVE | ALLOW_ANONYMOUS_OFFLINE (AppController.php:66).
Source code confirms CSRF is disabled: if ($action->id === 'migrate') { $this->enableCsrfValidation = false; } (AppController.php:92-94).
Source code confirms the action enables maintenance mode (line 301) and can trigger DB backups (line 307).
Docker build timed out during base image pull; reproduction was not executed.
limitations_or_blockers: Docker base image pull (php:8.2-cli, composer:2) did not complete within the session timeout due to network speed constraints.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit or ref, and mounts that checkout into the container.
